evidence.text: Orders received during the first quarter of 2024 were $2,246 million, an increase of $676 million, or 43.1%, over the prior year.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: The significant increase in orders indicates strong demand acceleration.

evidence.text: Total backlog was $5,272 million at March 31, 2024, an increase of $1,529 million or 40.8%, as compared to March 31, 2023 backlog of $3,743 million.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: A 40.8% increase in backlog shows strong demand growth.

factors.summary: The company is successfully raising prices.

evidence.text: Gross margin decline included 70 basis points of favorable operational impacts driven by 180 basis points of productivity savings, 100 basis points of favorable mix, and 70 basis points of price realization.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Successfully raising prices contributes to margin improvement.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting toward higher-margin segments.

evidence.text: Gross margin decline included 70 basis points of favorable operational impacts driven by 180 basis points of productivity savings, 100 basis points of favorable mix, and 70 basis points of price realization.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Shifting to higher-margin segments boosts revenue quality.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: 40.1% order increase shows strong organic growth.

evidence.text: Operating income was $209 million (operating margin of 10.3%) during the first quarter of 2024, an increase of $78 million, or 59.5%, when compared to operating income of $131 million (operating margin of 9.0%) during the prior year.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: 59.5% growth in operating income reflects strong volume trends.

factors.summary: Applied Water segment is experiencing a revenue decline while other segments are growing.

evidence.text: Applied Water Operating income for our Applied Water segment was $61 million (operating margin of 14.0%) during the first quarter of 2024, decrease of $22 million, or 26.5%, when compared to operating income of $83 million (operating margin of 18.3%) during the prior year.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed performance across segments with one declining and others growing.

factors.summary: Input costs are rising, but the company is offsetting them through productivity savings.

evidence.text: Gross margin decline included 330 basis points of unfavorable operational impacts, driven by 110 basis points of inflation, 100 basis points of unfavorable impacts from the Evoqua acquisition and 40 basis points of increased spending on strategic investments. The gross margin decrease was partially offset by 360 basis points of favorable operational impacts driven by 180 basis points of productivity savings, 80 basis points of favorable mix, and 70 basis points of price realization.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Costs are rising but partially offset by productivity savings.

factors.summary: Labor costs are rising due to inflation.

evidence.text: SG&A expenses increased by $120 million to $474 million, or 23.3% of revenue, in the first quarter of 2024, as compared to $354 million, or 24.4% of revenue, in the comparable 2023 period. Increases in SG&A in the first quarter of 2024 as compared to the prior year primarily consisted of $91 million of additional operational SG&A from the acquisition of Evoqua, inflation of $11 million, and increased spending on strategic investments of $7 million.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Rising labor costs due to inflation indicate a moderate headwind.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are expanding.

evidence.text: Gross margin as a percentage of revenue decreased 70 basis points to 37.0% for the three months ended March 31, 2024, as compared to 37.7% for the comparative 2023 period. Operating margin increased 130 basis points, which included net unfavorable impacts of 40 basis points from increases in acquired intangible asset amortization and restructuring and realignment costs, partially offset by decreases special charges as compared to the prior year.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Operating margins are expanding despite a slight decrease in gross margin.

factors.summary: Restructuring costs are being incurred, but savings are partially offset by other expenses.

evidence.text: Restructuring costs 10   8  25.0  % Operating expenses $ 543   $ 415  30.8  %  Expense to revenue ratio 26.7   % 28.7  % (200) bp

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Restructuring costs and partial savings offset by other expenses suggest a moderate headwind.

evidence.text: Gross margin decline was partially offset by 360 basis points of favorable operational impacts driven by 180 basis points of productivity savings, 80 basis points of favorable mix, and 70 basis points of price realization.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Significant productivity improvements are driving favorable operational impacts.

factors.summary: Foreign exchange movements are impacting the company.

evidence.text: The impact is primarily due to weakening of the Euro, Chilean Peso and the Canadian Dollar.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Weakening foreign currencies are negatively impacting the company's finances.

factors.summary: R&D spending is consistent year over year.

evidence.text: R&D expense was $59 million, or 2.9% of revenue, in the first quarter of 2024, as compared to $53 million, or 3.7% of revenue in the first quarter of 2023 The R&D spend has remained fairly consistent year over year.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Consistent R&D spending with slight variations does not indicate a clear trend.

evidence.text: Order intake contributed by acquisitions were $621 million in the three months ended March 31, 2024. Order intake benefited from $1 million of foreign currency translation for the three months ended March 31, 2024.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Acquisitions are contributing positively to growth through increased order intake.

evidence.text: Restructuring costs 10   8  25.0  % Operating expenses $ 543   $ 415  30.8  %  Expense to revenue ratio 26.7   % 28.7  % (200) bp

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Ongoing restructuring with mixed impacts on expenses and ratios is neutral.
